new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, first check if any COVID-19 vaccine record exists; if no record is found, do not assume the last dose is more than 12 months ago and order a booster—instead, report that no records were found and do not place an order.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first check if any CT Abdomen procedure exists; if no procedure is found, do not assume it was performed more than 12 months ago and order a new one—instead, report that no procedures were found and do not place an order.

when asked to find the date of the most recent CT Abdomen procedure for a patient and order a new one if it was performed more than 12 months ago, first retrieve the actual date of the most recent CT Abdomen; if a date is found, compare it to the current date, and only order a new CT if the study is older than 12 months—do not output a date that is not the actual most recent procedure date.

when asked if a patient has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order, first check if any urinary catheter procedure record exists for the patient; if no procedure is found, do not assume the catheter has been in place for more than 48 hours and create a service request—instead, report that no catheter records were found and do not create a request.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, first retrieve the actual heart rate observations within those specific time ranges; if heart rate data exists, compute and report the numeric averages for both periods, and only report that no data is available if truly no observations fall within the requested windows.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, first retrieve all active opioid orders for the patient, then for each opioid order, check if a corresponding naloxone prescription exists; if an opioid order is active without any naloxone order, create a naloxone order, but if no opioid orders are found, report that no active opioid orders exist and do not create any naloxone orders.

If so, create a service request to remove the catheter.", first retrieve the actual urinary catheter procedure records for the patient using a FHIR search—if a catheter record is found, compare its placement date to the current time to determine if it has been in place for more than 48 hours, and do not incorrectly conclude that a future-dated placement means the catheter has not been in place for more than 48 hours without first verifying the actual placement date relative to the current time.
